# **Day 1: 18/5/2026**

Attendance: Sean Corrigan(Tech Lead), Jerry Henderson(Quality Lead), Conor Faulkner(Scrum Master), Patrick Murphy(Product Owner)

## **What did we do?**

Did the daily plan and did introductions

## **Plan for the day?**

Set up fork, become familiar with codebase, become familiar with domain and the project brief, and what CK metrics

## **Plan for tomorrow**

Do the plan for sprint one, and start looking for errors

# **Day 2: 19/05/2026**

Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor,

### **What did we do?**

* Fork set up for Team Alpha.  
* Began to familiarise ourselves with the codebase and project brief  
  * Initial key findings/problems  
* Initial benchmarks

### **Plan for Day:**

* Begin to record intial findings/benchmarks  
* First Alpha team meeting  
  * Establish team roles  
  * Sprint 1 planning conducted  
  * Overview of each teams requirements/scope  
* Familiarse ourselves with codebase components relevant to persistance and workspace state.

### **Plan for tommorow:**

* Begin to research into inital findings/benchmarks  
* Attend relevant meetings  
* Review first ADR draft.  
* Scope out sub teams requirements and goals

# **Day 3: 20/05/2026**

Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor

### **What did we do?**

* Attended first team alpha meeting.  
* Got more familiar with codebase and began to pinpoint areas relevant to us.

### **Plan for the Day:**

* Send out google form to gather current state of other subteams  
* Begin to understand the problems pinpointed  
* Attend first tech lead meeting to go through ADR report.  
* Set up Kanban board

### **Plan for the Tommorow:**

* Attend relevant meetings  
* Identify Requirements \*\*\*\*  
* Discuss a major class relevant to us

# **Day 4 21/05/2026**

Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor, Patrick

### **What did we do?**

* Analysed problems pinpointed  
* ADR report discussed in Tech meeting  
* Kanban board for sprint established  
* Deep dive into relevant class began

### **Plan for day:**

* Attend scrum of scrums meeting  
* Draft requirements document  
* Begin to research what NEEDS to be done  
* Discuss findings from attended meetings  
* Request state contracts from sub teams

### **Plan for tommorow:**

* Review recieved state contracts  
* Begin to map out refactoring proposals  
* Conduct sprint review for week  
* Initial design proposals for interfaces

# **Day 5 22/05/2026:**

Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor

### **What did we do?**

* Scrum of scrums attended  
* Requirments doc drafted and discussed  
* Further research into problems within codebase

### **Plan for today:**

* Begin to propose refactoring examples  
* Sprint review and retro meetings  
* Discussion of our role within the overall project

### **What is blocking us?**

* No concrete architecture established for whole of team alpha  
* As our scope involves the majority of codebase, familiarisation with it all is proving difficult

# **Day 6 25/05/2026:**

Attendance: Sean (Scrum Master), Jerry(Product Owner), Conor(Tech Lead), Patrick(Quality Champion)

### **What did we do yesterday?**

* Attended Sprint Review and Retrospective meetings  
* Gathered a few state contracts  
* Met with other teams to discuss our role within their scope

### **Plan for today:**

* Completely refocus ourselves for the sprint ahead  
* Analyse our role within the architecture of the codebase  
* Meet with other teams for more state contracts

### **What is blocking us?**

* No team wide architecture established  
* Teams not sending out state contracts

# **Day 7 26/05/2026**

Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor.

### **What did we do yesterday?**

* Fully understood where we are relevant across the codebase and how we fit into the architecture  
* Met with other teams to help familiarise ourselves with how we interact with their scope

### **Plan for today:**

* Meet with remaining teams who have not provided a state contract  
* Finalise what our refactoring examples will be

### **What is blocking us?**

* Teams not present to meet for state contracts

**Day 8 27/05/2026**  
Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor,

**What did we do yesterday:**

* All state contracts acquired, analysed and understood  
* Method for completing refactored examples agreed upon

**Plan for today:**

* Carry out the implementation of our design for a persistence layer for the iDavie system  
* Create this layer with the help of Claude Code  
* Before any code implementation, plan and prepare the AI agent with .md files for context to ensure a proper implementation  
* After code creation, review and understand thoroughly

**What is blocking us today?**

* Some team members lack deep knowledge in how to prompt and prepare AI for a deep coding session

**Day 9 28/05/2026**  
Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor,

**What did we do yesterday:**

* Created persistence layer with help of claude code  
* Ensured its modularity and simplicity so it can easily be integrated

**Plan for today:**

* Carry out the tests for this created layer \- Unit and test in Unity itself (simulate crash)  
* Begin to create testing strategy doc

**What is blocking us today?**

* No blockings today

**Day 10 29/05/2026**  
Attendance: Sean, Jerry, Conor, Patrick

**What did we do yesterday:**

* Successfully tested the created persistence layer  
* Began to walkthrough new code to understand design choices and functionality

**Plan for today:**

* Completely focus on walkthrough of code

**What is blocking us today?**

* Nothing blocking us
